description: US-09 Reissue a Card After Loss — Replacement cards reuse the customer number with the next index; the old card stops working. No limit on reissues, but the count is visible so staff can judge.

userStories.title: reissueCard use case for a lost card
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to issue a replacement card in one action, so a customer who lost theirs can collect today. Source: tasks/prd-us-09-reissue-card-after-loss.md §US-09.1
acceptanceCriteria: reissueCard(deps, {customerId, reason: 'LOST'}) delegates to issueCard — there is one card-issuing code path, not two
The new index is the previous maximum + 1 and every earlier card becomes invalid as a consequence
Test asserts status, customer number, group, reminder count and distribution history are unchanged
No limit check exists — the tenth reissue succeeds exactly like the second
Rejects only for an archived customer
Writes an audit entry with reason LOST
Tested against hand-written fakes, including two reissues in a row
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reissue counts on the customer read model
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to see how many cards a customer has been issued, so I can judge whether they are losing them unusually often. Source: tasks/prd-us-09-reissue-card-after-loss.md §US-09.2
acceptanceCriteria: The customer read model exposes cardsIssued (the current index) and reissuesForLoss (count of cards with reason LOST)
The two are reported separately so a stale-counts reissue is not counted against the customer as a loss
The repository query is a single aggregate, not a load-all-and-count in application code
Integration test seeds a mixed history (FIRST_ISSUE, LOST, STALE_COUNTS, LOST) and asserts cardsIssued = 4 and reissuesForLoss = 2
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Card reissue UI
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to reissue a card from the card view or the customer record, with the count visible. Source: tasks/prd-us-09-reissue-card-after-loss.md §US-09.3
acceptanceCriteria: 'Karte neu ausstellen (Verlust)' action on the card view and the customer detail page
A confirmation step states the old number and the number that will be issued, before writing
After reissue the card view shows the new number and states that all earlier numbers are invalid
The card view shows the reissue count with loss-caused reissues called out separately
The system never warns, blocks or nags about a high count — it only displays it
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: old card refused, new card works
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving reissue invalidates the old card without penalising the customer. Source: tasks/prd-us-09-reissue-card-after-loss.md §US-09.4
acceptanceCriteria: Spec reissues a card for a customer holding Nk1 and asserts the new number is Nk2
Looking up Nk1 asserts the German invalid-card verdict naming Nk2, and that no distribution record, block or archive resulted
Looking up Nk2 asserts clear to serve
Spec reissues twice more and asserts the displayed reissue count reaches 3
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
